zone_6b_notes: Direct sow or transplant after last frost — May 1–15 in Farmington. Butternut is a long-season crop requiring the full Missouri growing season (100+ frost-free days). Needs room to sprawl — vines reach 10–15 feet; plant at garden edge or train along fence. Harvest after first light frost when skin is tan and hardens to resist fingernail penetration. Cure at 80–85°F for 10 days to develop sweetness; stores 3–6 months at room temperature. Vine borers are the primary threat in Missouri — use row cover until flowering, then remove to allow pollination.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow May 1–15 or transplant after last frost

water: consistent — 1 inch/week; avoid wetting leaves to reduce mildew
